name: AIDA: Marketing Funnel
summary: The classic customer journey model: Attention, Interest, Desire, Action — how people move from not knowing you to buying from you.
whenToUseIt: Use AIDA when planning campaigns, writing copy, designing landing pages, or mapping out your sales funnel. It helps ensure your marketing addresses each stage of decision-making.
steps: Attention: Capture their attention — bold headlines, striking visuals, pattern interrupts, SEO visibility
Interest: Build their interest — relevant content, storytelling, problem identification, social proof
Desire: Create desire — show benefits (not just features), testimonials, demos, emotional triggers
Action: Drive action — clear CTA, reduced friction, urgency, guarantees, easy checkout
example: A Facebook ad for a fitness app: Attention = 'Lost 15kg in 12 weeks — here's how' with a before/after image. Interest = '3 meals a day, no gym required, backed by nutritionists.' Desire = video testimonials from real users with similar starting points. Action = 'Start your free 7-day trial — no credit card required.'
